page: Amazon Web Services c8ine.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by dedicated Intel x86_64 processors. It features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance type does not include local storage, requiring external storage volumes for persistent data operations. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8ine.4xlarge offers a dense compute profile with dedicated CPU allocation, making it suitable for workloads requiring consistent high-frequency processing without the overhead of local storage. Appropriate workloads include compute-intensive applications, network-optimized tasks, batch processing, and distributed analytics that leverage external storage architectures.
description: Amazon Web Services c8ine.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel processors. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. Designed for compute-heavy and network-optimized tasks, the c8ine.4xlarge is structured for workloads such as batch processing, high-performance web serving, and distributed analytics where dedicated CPU performance and high network throughput are required, while avoiding the cost overhead of bundled local storage.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 compute-optimized configuration featuring high-frequency Intel processors and high-speed network connectivity for intensive processing workloads.
